runType: growth-expert run (Firecrawl-powered: live 2026 algo research + graded Firestore records)
algoRulesApplied: Replies weighted ~27x likes in 2026 — every candidate ends on a genuine, answerable question (not 'thoughts?')
First 30 min velocity decides reach — post hero at a peak window, self-reply with the data point at ~25 min
Links in tweet 1 cut reach ~50% — site link ONLY in the self-reply
No hashtags (3+ triggers spam filter; semantic NLP categorizes content now)

today.spain: Spain ML -295, 5u TOP+ ELITE, 3:00 PM ET, proven winners 2 for / 0 against (unanimous)
today.portugal: Portugal ML -135, 3u, 7:00 PM ET, proven winners 6 for / 1 against (loudest consensus today)

verifiedNumbers.timingFlag: Both games >60 min out at write time — numbers flagged as 'can still move'

hero.structure: confession-flex story (NEW architecture — not the bullet card)
hero.text:
We're 16-5 betting the World Cup and I couldn't name Austria's starting XI.

We don't watch the games. We watch the wallets — a small group of bettors who win year after year, and where their money goes.

Knockout round today, and they're loud:

Spain, 3pm ET — our top play. Every proven winner we track is on it. Zero against.
Portugal, 7pm ET — 6 winning wallets in, 1 fading.

+17 units in three weeks doing exactly this.

So tell me — is not watching the games a red flag, or is it the whole point?
hero.rtLine: We're 16-5 betting the World Cup and I couldn't name Austria's starting XI.
whyItWins: Line 1 passes the screenshot test cold: flex + self-deprecation + curiosity gap in one sentence. It reads human, not templated.
The close is a genuinely polarizing, answerable question — purists will say fraud, quants will defend it. Both reply. Replies are 27x.
Record-on-an-angle (16-5 World Cup) is the niche's most believable flex shape; marquee teams (Spain, Portugal) keep it legible.
Structure rotation: story/confession, NOT the record+bullets card we've run 4x.
hero.selfReplyAt25min:
The wallets aren't split today, for what it's worth. Spain is unanimous — every proven winner we track, zero against. Portugal is 6-to-1. When they disagree with each other we stay small. When they line up like this we don't overthink it.

Full card + the tracker: nhlsavant.com
hero.postWindow: 11:30 AM – 12:15 PM ET (lunch scroll, ~3.5h before Spain kicks — urgency without being stale)

alternates.text:
Yesterday: 5-7. Baseball bit us.

World Cup card: still 16-5, +17 units since group play.

So today we're back on the soccer side of the house. Spain at 3, Portugal at 7 — both backed by the winning wallets we track, Spain unanimously.

We post the Ls. That's why you can trust the Ws.

Riding the soccer run with us, or waiting for it to end?
alternates.rtLine: We post the Ls. That's why you can trust the Ws.
alternates.note: Use this if you'd rather lead with accountability after yesterday's red day. Identity beat + record + plays in one.

alternates.text:
Two knockout games today. The winning wallets we track are on the favorite in both — but on one of them, they're completely unanimous. Not one proven winner on the other side.

Spain or Portugal. Which one do you think it is?

Replies get the answer + the full read at 1pm ET.
alternates.rtLine: Not one proven winner on the other side.
alternates.note: Max reply-forcer. Requires the 1pm follow-through post (answer: Spain, 2-0 unanimous, our 5u top play).

doNotDo: No bullet-card 'record + picks + tail or fade? 🎯' template — retired
No precise wallet $ figures — directional only
Don't quote Spain/Portugal numbers after ~1pm without re-pulling the board — flagged as movable
